steps.title: Identify the standard circle equation form
steps.content: The equation $(x + 6)^2 + (y + 3)^2 = 121$ is in the standard form of a circle equation: $$(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$$ where $(h, k)$ is the center and $r$ is the radius. The constant on the right side equals $r^2$.

steps.title: Solve for the radius
steps.content: From our equation, $r^2 = 121$. Taking the square root of both sides: $$r = \sqrt{121} = 11$$
